nodes.label: Three-Tier Graduated Safety System
nodes.rationale: Implements SECURE (read-only), ANALYSIS (limited write), and DEVELOPMENT (full access) tiers to provide graduated access control for SQL Server operations via MCP. Defaults to least-privilege to prevent accidental data mutations in production, while enabling flexible write access in controlled development environments. This is the primary architectural safety mechanism for the entire server.

nodes.label: Dependabot Auto-Merge Decision Policy
nodes.rationale: Security-critical and production database connectivity dependencies (mssql, tedious, @azure/identity, aws-sdk, codeql-action) require manual review. Dev/test/docs tooling and patch/minor semver bumps are auto-merged to reduce maintenance overhead while protecting core functionality from unreviewed changes.

nodes.label: Conservative Security Approach for SSL Certificate Trust

nodes.label: Three-Tier Safety System
nodes.rationale: Three-tier safety system (read-only mode, DML restrictions, DDL restrictions) is the primary security architecture, and tests are described as the 'last line of defense' against accidental data loss or malicious attacks.

nodes.label: Test Mock Strategy
nodes.rationale: Tests use comprehensive mocking (mssql module, connection pool, request objects) to ensure fast execution without real SQL Server, reliable cross-environment results, and isolated test runs.

nodes.label: Modular Test Organization
nodes.rationale: Tests organized by functional area (connection, security, core tools, data tools, performance, lifecycle) to enable faster development iteration, isolated testing, and parallel execution in CI/CD.

nodes.label: MCP Client Smoke Test (JS file reference)
nodes.norm_label: mcp client smoke test (js file reference)

nodes.label: Test Isolation Strategy
nodes.rationale: Test isolation involves unique database name prefixes per run, complete cleanup between suites, and orphaned database detection to prevent cross-test contamination.

nodes.label: Intelligent Platform Detection System
nodes.rationale: Automatically detects hardware architecture and Docker capabilities to choose the optimal SQL Server container configuration, eliminating platform mismatch warnings and reducing onboarding friction.

nodes.label: Docker vs Manual Testing Trade-off
nodes.rationale: Docker provides faster setup (2-3 min vs 30+ min), consistent environments, and automatic cleanup but sacrifices production-like enterprise features, version flexibility, and Windows Authentication. Manual setup is recommended for pre-release production validation.

nodes.label: Manual-Only CI/CD Exclusion Rationale
nodes.rationale: Integration tests require live SQL Server, specific environment config, and production-like setup, making them unsuitable for automated CI/CD pipelines. They are intentionally excluded to avoid flaky CI builds.
